Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Lindsey Kernan appearing via zoom for Whittier Trust Company (petitioner).
Jim Cummins appearing via zoom for Harry Litke (beneficiary).

Mrs. Kernan stated that the attorney in Montana was a longtime counsel and was a part of his life for decades. The Court hearing no objections. First amended petition for (1) Reformation of Trust or, in the Alternative, (2) Modification of January 15, 2021 order is taken under submission with the intent to grant the trust reformation as prayed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Chavez. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laurelle Gutierrez appeared by telephone for Stefano Casalino ((Attorney-in-Fact for Margherita Litke).
Christina Gallagher Nelson for Jeffrey Litke (Co-Trustee/Remainder Beneficiary).
John Flegel appeared by zoom for Cynthia Hacker (Beneficiary/Former Trustee).
Paul Barulich appeared by zoom for Harry Litke (Co-Trustee).
Geoffrey Cohen appeared by zoom for and with Thomas Frank (by zoom) from Whittier Trust.

The Court noted that the trust is not under Court supervision and stated that it looks like the parties have worked in good faith to come to this solution. Ms. Nelson stated that there are no objections, that the parties have worked hard, that they had several mediations and that all parties are in agreement. Mr. Barulich stated that the fees were for the prior Trustees who are the adult children and served for three years, that they had not paid themselves during that time, spoke of the value of the trust and stated that the only party affected by this is the income beneficiary (Margherita). Mr. Gutierrez stated that she agrees with everything Counsel said, that they had multiple mediations, that several properties were sold and that there is no concern about the level of fees. The Court informed the parties that there will be some small modifications to the proposed order. Mr. Cohen stated that if the Court is inclined to modify the order, that he would like the opportunity to review the modifications prior to the Court signing it. The Court declined Mr. Cohen's request. The Petition for Order to Appoint Corporate Successor Trustee and the Petition to Remove Co-Trustees are under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To May-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Flegel for Cynthia Hacker, Respondent, via Court Call.
Laurelle Gutierrez for Stefano Casalini, attorney in fact for Margarita Litke.
Christina Nelson for and with Jeff Litke, Co-Trustee/Beneficiary.
Paul Barulich and Jim Cummins for Harry Litke, Trustee.

Ms. Gutierrez stated that parties had a proposal from 10/15/19 mediation that could have resolved this matter but there were adverse tax consequences to Magherita Litke that were not presented to her at the time so the issue of getting a corporate trustee in place is not resolved and further stated that Mr. Barulich spoke with a representative from Wells Fargo who would step in as Trustee. Mr. Barulich stated this is the first he is hearing about adverse tax consequences and that would be between Jeff and Margherita, that Wells Fargo would serve condition less, and that Northern Trust has conditions that extend 1 year. The Court noted it will not sign an order unless it is agreed by all parties, that the best alternative is for parties to work together to find a fiduciary to step in. Mr. Barulich stated that he knows this case has been continued too many times and requested the court's patience for a 30-day continuance as parties are inclined to move in that direction. Ms. Nelson stated her client is managing everything and using his own staff, that she didn't hear of Wells Fargo's interest and would need to see if they are totally unconditional as other professionals have conditions. The Court noted that it is not encouraging that some parties are hearing about Wells Fargo for the first time today and skeptical they will serve without conditions. Ms. Gutierrez stated there is litigation in Montana for Northern Trust.
